checklist.action: write analysis to .pipeline-state/<branch>-analysis.md so later stages can read it
preserve ALL existing behavior — no functional changes
if analysis specifies tests, write them — refactors that change structure need tests proving behavior is preserved
cross-ref analysis test list against actual tests written
write list of all changed files to .pipeline-state/<branch>-changes.txt (git diff --name-only HEAD)
run full test suite (read-only, no source modifications)
cross-ref analysis plan vs refactoring — flag unimplemented changes
cd terraform && terraform fmt -check -recursive on staged files
read .pipeline-state/<branch>-analysis.md to understand what was refactored
discover project docs structure (read docs/README.md or index)
add/update docstrings for modified public APIs
update existing docs that reference moved/renamed code
verify no broken internal links or orphaned docs

if this change adds/renames/removes an enumerated unit (skill, CLI/slash command, plugin, make target, item in a 'supported X' list), update EVERY inventory/index doc that lists its siblings — grep a sibling's name across non-source files to find them; DOCS_ONLY does not exempt this
read .pipeline-state/<branch>-changes.txt for list of files modified by refactor
git remote -v — verify github remote exists
git add -u to stage ALL modified tracked files
git add each file from changes.txt to catch new files
verify PR body against actual diff — no phantom features, wrong counts, or incorrect terminology; fix with gh pr edit if needed

9.name: Code Review
9.subagent_prompt: You are reviewing PR #{pr_number} for a refactor. Project: {project_path}. PR targets {pr_target_branch}. Step 0: Read .pipeline-state/*-analysis.md for the original analysis. Step 1: Run git diff {pr_target_branch}...HEAD, read changed files. Check docs/PULL_REQUEST_CHECKLIST.md if exists. Step 2: Cross-ref analysis vs implementation — verify all planned changes were made. CRITICAL: Verify behavior is preserved — refactor must NOT change functionality. Step 3: Review for correctness, security, testing, quality, docs, perf. Flag auto-reject triggers. Step 4: Run project test suite, report pass/fail. Step 5: Decide APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT. Step 6 MANDATORY: Post review to GitHub with gh pr review {pr_number} --comment --body '<formatted review>'. If REQUEST_CHANGES use --request-changes. Save to pr/feedback/ if dir exists. Step 7: Output verdict. Step FINAL (MANDATORY, #36): you are read-only — restore the executor's working tree before returning. Capture your starting branch first (START=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)); on exit run `git checkout "$START"` and verify `git status --porcelain` is clean (`git restore --staged --worktree .` + `git clean -fd` (remove untracked files you created) if you dirtied it). NEVER leave the tree on {pr_target_branch} or with reverted/staged files — a build/scp the executor runs next would ship stale content.

checklist.action: load project PR checklist if exists (docs/PULL_REQUEST_CHECKLIST.md)
cross-ref analysis vs implementation — all planned changes made
verify behavior preserved — refactor must NOT change functionality
review: correctness, security, testing, quality, docs, perf
run full test suite, report pass/fail
post review to GitHub: gh pr review --comment with formatted checklist and findings
save to pr/feedback/ if directory exists

10.name: Review Verdict
10.note: Verdict is determined by the Code Review subagent above. If APPROVE, proceed to Merge. If REQUEST_CHANGES, fix issues, re-commit, re-push, re-run Code Review.

checklist.action: if REQUEST_CHANGES: fix issues, re-commit, re-push, re-run stage 9

11.name: Merge PR
11.subagent_prompt: Merge PR #{pr_number} in {project_path}. Step 0: Run 'gh pr checks {pr_number}' — verify CI green. If pre-existing failure, document with gh pr comment before merging. Step 1: Run gh pr merge {pr_number} --squash --delete-branch. Do NOT run gh pr review --approve (GitHub blocks self-approval). Output PR_MERGED or MERGE_FAILED.

checklist.action: verify CI green: gh pr checks — if failing for pre-existing reasons, document in PR comment before merging

12.name: Retrospective
12.subagent_prompt: Write retrospective for refactor: {task_description}. Project: {project_path}. PR #{pr_number}. Run 'gh pr view {pr_number} --json commits,title,body' to get PR details (branch deleted after merge). Rate quality 1-5, what went well, lessons learned, improvements. MANDATORY: Post retro as gh pr comment {pr_number} --body '<formatted retro with quality, lessons, improvements>'. MANDATORY: Append to .pipeline-log.md (create if needed, ensure in .gitignore). Your FINAL output line MUST be exactly 'RETRO_COMPLETE'.

checklist.action: post retrospective to GitHub PR: gh pr comment
